chunks.text: Anca Dragan is an Associate Professor at UC Berkeley EECS. She runs the InterACT lab, focusing on enabling AI agents and robots to work with, around, and in support of people. One core problem is AI alignment, getting agents to do what people actually want by interactively learning from human feedback and modeling human decision-making. The lab also works on algorithms for human-AI collaboration, enabling agents to fluently assist humans in physical or virtual tasks.

Anca Dragan earned a PhD in Robotics from Carnegie Mellon University in 2015 and a B.S. in Computer Science from Jacobs University, Bremen in 2009. Her research areas include Artificial Intelligence (AI), Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR), and Human-Computer Interaction (HCI).

Anca Dragan is affiliated with several research centers: Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research Lab (BAIR), Berkeley Deep Drive (BDD), Berkeley Institute of Design (BID), Center for Human Compatible Artificial Intelligence (CHAI), Center for Information Technology Research in the Interest of Society - The Banatao Institute (CITRIS), and the Robotics and Intelligent Machines Laboratory.

Selected honors and awards include: RAS Early Career Award (2021), Diane S. McEntyre Award for Excellence in Teaching Computer Science (2020), NSF Presidential Early Career Award for Scientists & Engineers (PECASE) (2019), Sloan Research Fellow (2018), MIT Tech Review Top 35 Innovators Under 35 (TR35) (2017), NSF Faculty Early Career Development Award (CAREER) (2017), and Okawa Research Grant (2017).
